234	@dataclass(frozen=True)  # hashable
235	class PyosysType:
236	    """
237	    Bit of a hacky object all-around: this is more or less used to encapsulate
238	    container types so they can be later made opaque using pybind.
239	    """
240	
241	    base: str
242	    specialization: Tuple["PyosysType", ...]
243	    const: bool = False
244	
245	    @classmethod
246	    def from_type(Self, type_obj, drop_const=False) -> "PyosysType":
247	        const = hasattr(type_obj, "const") and type_obj.const and not drop_const
248	        if isinstance(type_obj, Pointer):
249	            ptr_to = Self.from_type(type_obj.ptr_to)
250	            return Self("ptr", (ptr_to,), const)
251	        elif isinstance(type_obj, Reference):
252	            ref_to = Self.from_type(type_obj.ref_to)
253	            return Self("ref", (ref_to,), const)
254	        elif isinstance(type_obj, FunctionType):
255	            ret_type = Self.from_type(type_obj.return_type)
256	            param_types = (Self.from_type(p.type) for p in type_obj.parameters)
257	            return Self("fn", (ret_type, *param_types), False)
258	        assert isinstance(
259	            type_obj, Type
260	        ), f"unexpected c++ type object of type {type(type_obj)}"
261	        last_segment = type_obj.typename.segments[-1]
262	        base = last_segment.name
263	        specialization = tuple()
264	        if (
265	            hasattr(last_segment, "specialization")
266	            and last_segment.specialization is not None
267	        ):
268	            for template_arg in last_segment.specialization.args:
269	                specialization = (*specialization, Self.from_type(template_arg.arg))
270	        return Self(base, specialization, const)
271	
272	    def generate_identifier(self):
273	        title = self.base.title()
274	        if len(self.specialization) == 0:
275	            return title
276	
277	        if title == "Dict":
278	            key, value = self.specialization
279	            return f"{key.generate_identifier()}To{value.generate_identifier()}{title}"
280	        elif title == "Fn":
281	            identifier = self.specialization[0].generate_identifier()
282	            if identifier == "Void":
283	                identifier = ""
284	            else:
285	                identifier += "From"
286	            identifier += "And".join(
287	                p.generate_identifier() for p in self.specialization[1:]
288	            )
289	            return identifier
290	
291	        return (
292	            "".join(spec.generate_identifier() for spec in self.specialization) + title
293	        )
294	
295	    def generate_cpp_name(self):
296	        const_prefix = "const " * self.const
297	        if len(self.specialization) == 0:
298	            return const_prefix + self.base
299	        elif self.base == "ptr":
300	            return const_prefix + f"{self.specialization[0].generate_cpp_name()} *"
301	        elif self.base == "ref":
302	            return const_prefix + f"{self.specialization[0].generate_cpp_name()} &"
303	        elif self.base == "fn":
304	            param_cpp_names = (s.generate_cpp_name() for s in self.specialization[1:])
305	            return f"{self.specialization[0].generate_cpp_name()}({','.join(param_cpp_names)})"
306	        else:
307	            return (
308	                const_prefix
309	                + f"{self.base}<{', '.join(spec.generate_cpp_name() for spec in self.specialization)}>"
310	            )
311	
312	
313	class PyosysWrapperGenerator(object):
314	    def __init__(
315	        self,
316	        from_headers: Iterable[PyosysHeader],
317	        wrapper_stream: io.TextIOWrapper,
318	        header_stream: io.TextIOWrapper,
319	    ):
320	        self.headers = from_headers
321	        self.f = wrapper_stream
322	        self.f_inc = header_stream
323	        self.found_containers: Dict[PyosysType, Any] = {}
324	        self.class_registry: Dict[str, Tuple[ClassScope, PyosysClass]] = {}
325	
326	    # entry point
327	    def generate(self, build_dir):
328	        tpl = __file_dir__ / "wrappers_tpl.cc"
329	        preprocessor_opts = self.make_preprocessor_options(build_dir)
330	        with open(tpl, encoding="utf8") as f:
331	            do_line_directive = True
332	            for i, line in enumerate(f):
333	                if do_line_directive:
334	                    self.f.write(f'#line {i + 1} "{tpl}"\n')
335	                    do_line_directive = False
336	                if "<!-- generated includes -->" in line:
337	                    for header in self.headers:
338	                        self.f.write(f'#include "{header.name}"\n')
339	                    do_line_directive = True
340	                elif "<!-- generated pymod-level code -->" in line:
341	                    for header in self.headers:
342	                        header_path = __yosys_root__ / header.name
343	                        parsed = parse_file(header_path, options=preprocessor_opts)
344	                        global_namespace = parsed.namespace
345	                        self.process_namespace(header, global_namespace)
346	                else:
347	                    self.f.write(line)
348	
349	        for container, _ in self.found_containers.items():
350	            identifier = container.generate_identifier()
351	            print(
352	                f"using {identifier} = {container.generate_cpp_name()};",
353	                file=self.f_inc,
354	            )
355	            print(f"PYBIND11_MAKE_OPAQUE({identifier})", file=self.f_inc)
356	
357	        print(
358	            f"static void bind_autogenerated_opaque_containers(py::module &m) {{",
359	            file=self.f_inc,
360	        )
361	        for container, _ in self.found_containers.items():
362	            identifier = container.generate_identifier()
363	            cxx = container.generate_cpp_name()
364	            tpl_args = [cxx]
365	            for spec in container.specialization:
366	                tpl_args.append(spec.generate_cpp_name())
367	            print(
368	                f'\tpy::hashlib::bind_{container.base}<{", ".join(tpl_args)}>(m, "{container.generate_identifier()}");',
369	                file=self.f_inc,
370	            )
371	            print(
372	                f"\tpy::implicitly_convertible<py::iterable, {identifier}>();",
373	                file=self.f_inc,
374	            )
375	        print(f"}}", file=self.f_inc)
376	
377	    # helpers
378	    def make_preprocessor_options(self, build_dir):
379	        py_include = get_paths()["include"]
380	        cxx_bin = os.getenv("CXX") or shutil.which("clang++") or "c++"
381	        if ccache := os.getenv("CCACHE"):
382	            compiler = [ccache, cxx_bin]
383	        else:
384	            compiler = [cxx_bin]
385	        cxx_std = os.getenv("CXX_STD", "c++20")
386	        return ParserOptions(
387	            preprocessor=make_gcc_preprocessor(
388	                defines=["_YOSYS_", "YOSYS_ENABLE_PYTHON"],
389	                gcc_args=[*compiler, f"-std={cxx_std}", "-fsyntax-only"],
390	                include_paths=[str(__yosys_root__), py_include, pybind11.get_include(), build_dir],
391	            ),
392	        )
393	
394	    @staticmethod
395	    def find_containers(
396	        containers: Iterable[str], type_info: Any
397	    ) -> Dict[PyosysType, Any]:
398	        if isinstance(type_info, Pointer):
399	            return PyosysWrapperGenerator.find_containers(containers, type_info.ptr_to)
400	        if isinstance(type_info, MoveReference):
401	            return PyosysWrapperGenerator.find_containers(
402	                containers, type_info.moveref_to
403	            )
404	        if isinstance(type_info, Reference):
405	            return PyosysWrapperGenerator.find_containers(containers, type_info.ref_to)
406	        if not isinstance(type_info, Type):
407	            return {}
408	        segments = type_info.typename.segments
409	        containers_found = {}
410	        for segment in segments:
411	            if isinstance(segment, FundamentalSpecifier):
412	                continue
413	            if segment.name in containers:
414	                containers_found.update(
415	                    {PyosysType.from_type(type_info, drop_const=True): type_info}
416	                )
417	            if segment.specialization is not None:
418	                for arg in segment.specialization.args:
419	                    sub_containers = PyosysWrapperGenerator.find_containers(
420	                        containers, arg.arg
421	                    )
422	                    containers_found.update(sub_containers)
423	        return containers_found
424	
425	    @staticmethod
426	    def find_anonymous_union(cls: ClassScope):
427	        if cls.class_decl.typename.classkey != "union":
428	            return None
429	        for s in cls.class_decl.typename.segments:
430	            if isinstance(s, AnonymousName):
431	                return s
432	        return None  # named union
433	
434	    @staticmethod
435	    def get_parameter_types(function: Function) -> str:
436	        return ", ".join(p.type.format() for p in function.parameters)
437	
438	    def register_containers(self, target: Union[Function, Field, Variable]) -> bool:
439	        supported = ("dict", "idict", "pool", "set", "vector")
440	        found = False
441	        if isinstance(target, Function):
442	            return_type_containers = self.find_containers(supported, target.return_type)
443	            found = found or len(return_type_containers)
444	            self.found_containers.update(return_type_containers)
445	
446	            for parameter in target.parameters:
447	                parameter_containers = self.find_containers(supported, parameter.type)
448	                found = found or len(parameter_containers)
449	                self.found_containers.update(parameter_containers)
450	        else:
451	            variable_containers = self.find_containers(supported, target.type)
452	            found = found or len(variable_containers)
453	            self.found_containers.update(variable_containers)
454	        return found
455	
456	    # processors
457	    def get_overload_cast(
458	        self, function: Function, class_basename: Optional[str]
459	    ) -> str:
460	        is_method = isinstance(function, Method)
461	        function_return_type = function.return_type.format()
462	        if class_basename in {"Const","IdString"} and function_return_type in {
463	            "iterator",
464	            "const_iterator",
465	        }:
466	            # HACK: qualify Const's iterators
467	            function_return_type = f"{class_basename}::{function_return_type}"
468	
469	        pointer_kind = (
470	            f"{class_basename}::*" if (is_method and not function.static) else "*"
471	        )
472	
473	        retval = f"static_cast <"
474	        retval += function_return_type
475	        retval += f"({pointer_kind})"
476	        retval += f"({self.get_parameter_types(function)})"
477	        if is_method and function.const:
478	            retval += " const"
479	        retval += ">"
480	        retval += "(&"
481	        if is_method:
482	            retval += f"{class_basename}::"
483	        retval += function.name.segments[-1].format()
484	        retval += ")"
485	        return retval
486	
487	    def get_definition_args(
488	        self,
489	        function: Function,
490	        class_basename: Optional[str],
491	        python_name_override: Optional[str] = None,
492	    ) -> List[str]:
493	        function_basename = function.name.segments[-1].format()
494	
495	        python_function_basename = python_name_override or keyword_aliases.get(
496	            function_basename, function_basename
497	        )
498	
499	        def_args = [f'"{python_function_basename}"']
500	        def_args.append(self.get_overload_cast(function, class_basename))
501	        for i, parameter in enumerate(function.parameters):
502	            name = parameter.name or f"arg{i}"
503	            parameter_arg = f'py::arg("{name}")'
504	            if parameter.default is not None:
505	                parameter_arg += f" = {parameter.default.format()}"
506	            def_args.append(parameter_arg)
507	
508	        return def_args
509	
510	    def process_method(self, metadata: PyosysClass, function: Method):
511	        if (
512	            function.deleted
513	            or function.template
514	            or function.vararg
515	            or function.access != "public"
516	            or function.pure_virtual
517	            or function.destructor
518	        ):
519	            return
520	
521	        if any(isinstance(p.type, MoveReference) for p in function.parameters):
522	            # skip move constructors
523	            return
524	
525	        if len(function.name.segments) > 1:
526	            # can't handle, skip
527	            return
528	
529	        if function.constructor:
530	            if (
531	                not metadata.ref_only
532	            ):  # ref-only classes should not be constructed from python
533	                print(
534	                    f"\t\t\t.def(py::init<{self.get_parameter_types(function)}>())",
535	                    file=self.f,
536	                )
537	            return
538	
539	        python_name_override = None
540	        if function.operator is not None:
541	            if function.operator == "==":
542	                python_name_override = "__eq__"
543	            elif function.operator == "!=":
544	                python_name_override = "__ne__"
545	            elif function.operator == "<":
546	                python_name_override = "__lt__"
547	            elif function.operator == "[]" and function.const:
548	                python_name_override = "__getitem__"
549	            else:
550	                return
551	
552	        self.register_containers(function)
553	
554	        definition_fn = "def"
555	        if function.static:
556	            definition_fn = "def_static"
557	
558	        definition_args = self.get_definition_args(
559	            function, metadata.name, python_name_override
560	        )
561	
562	        print(
563	            f"\t\t\t.{definition_fn}({', '.join(definition_args)})",
564	            file=self.f,
565	        )
566	
567	    def process_function(self, function: Function):
568	        if function.deleted or function.template or function.vararg or function.static:
569	            return
570	
571	        if function.operator is not None:
572	            # Python doesn't do global operators
573	            return
574	
575	        if function.name.segments[-1].format() in global_denylist:
576	            return
577	
578	        self.register_containers(function)
579	
580	        print(
581	            f"\t\t\tm.def({', '.join(self.get_definition_args(function, None))});",
582	            file=self.f,
583	        )
584	
585	    def process_field(self, metadata: PyosysClass, field: Field):
586	        if field.access != "public":
587	            return
588	
589	        if field.name is None:
590	            # anonymous structs and unions
591	            # unions handled in calling function
592	            # ASSUMPTION: No anonymous structs in the yosys codebase
593	            # 			  (they're not in the C++ standard anyway)
594	            return
595	
596	        unique_ptrs = self.find_containers(("unique_ptr",), field.type)
597	        if len(unique_ptrs):
598	            # TODO: figure out how to bridge unique pointers maybe does anyone
599	            # care
600	            return
601	
602	        self.register_containers(field)
603	        rvp = "py::return_value_policy::copy"
604	        if isinstance(field.type, Pointer):
605	            rvp = "py::return_value_policy::reference_internal"
606	
607	        definition_fn = f"def_{'readonly' if field.type.const else 'readwrite'}"
608	        if field.static:
609	            definition_fn += "_static"
610	
611	        field_python_basename = keyword_aliases.get(field.name, field.name)
612	
613	        def_args = [
614	            f'"{field_python_basename}"',
615	            f"&{metadata.name}::{field.name}",
616	        ]
617	        def_args.append(rvp)
618	        print(
619	            f"\t\t\t.{definition_fn}({', '.join(def_args)})",
620	            file=self.f,
621	        )
622	
623	    def process_variable(self, variable: Variable):
624	        if isinstance(variable.type, Array):
625	            return
626	
627	        variable_basename = variable.name.segments[-1].format()
628	        if variable_basename in global_denylist:
629	            return
630	
631	        self.register_containers(variable)
632	
633	        definition_fn = (
634	            f"def_{'readonly' if (variable.type.const or variable.constexpr) else 'readwrite'}_static"
635	        )
636	
637	        variable_python_basename = keyword_aliases.get(
638	            variable_basename, variable_basename
639	        )
640	        variable_name = variable.name.format()
641	
642	        print(
643	            f'\t\t\tglobal_variables.{definition_fn}("{variable_python_basename}", &{variable_name});',
644	            file=self.f,
645	        )
646	
647	    def process_class_members(
648	        self,
649	        metadata: PyosysClass,
650	        base_metadata: PyosysClass,
651	        cls: ClassScope,
652	        basename: str,
653	    ):
654	        for method in cls.methods:
655	            if method.name.segments[-1].name in base_metadata.denylist:
656	                continue
657	            self.process_method(metadata, method)
658	
659	        visited_anonymous_unions = set()
660	        for field_ in cls.fields:
661	            if field_.name in base_metadata.denylist:
662	                continue
663	            self.process_field(metadata, field_)
664	
665	            # Handle anonymous unions
666	            for subclass in cls.classes:
667	                if subclass.class_decl.access != "public":
668	                    continue
669	                if au := self.find_anonymous_union(subclass):
670	                    if au.id in visited_anonymous_unions:
671	                        continue
672	                    visited_anonymous_unions.add(au.id)
673	                    for subfield in subclass.fields:
674	                        self.process_field(metadata, subfield)
675	
676	        for base in cls.class_decl.bases:
677	            if base.access != "public":
678	                continue
679	            name = base.typename.segments[-1].format()
680	            if processed := self.class_registry.get(name):
681	                base_scope, base_metadata = processed
682	                self.process_class_members(
683	                    metadata, base_metadata, base_scope, basename
684	                )
685	
686	    def process_class(
687	        self,
688	        metadata: PyosysClass,
689	        cls: ClassScope,
690	        namespace_components: Tuple[str, ...],
691	    ):
692	        pqname: PQName = cls.class_decl.typename
693	        full_path = list(namespace_components) + [
694	            segment.format() for segment in pqname.segments
695	        ]
696	        basename = full_path.pop()
697	        self.class_registry[basename] = (cls, metadata)
698	
699	        declaration_namespace = "::".join(full_path)
700	        tpl_args = [basename]
701	        if metadata.ref_only:
702	            tpl_args.append(f"std::unique_ptr<{basename}, py::nodelete>")
703	        print(
704	            f'\t\t{{using namespace {declaration_namespace}; py::class_<{", ".join(tpl_args)}>(m, "{basename}")',
705	            file=self.f,
706	        )
707	
708	        self.process_class_members(metadata, metadata, cls, basename)
709	
710	        if basename == "Design":
711	            print(
712	                '\t\t\t.def("run_pass", [](Design &s, std::vector<std::string> cmd) { Pass::call(&s, cmd); })',
713	                file=self.f,
714	            )
715	            print(
716	                '\t\t\t.def("run_pass", [](Design &s, std::string cmd) { Pass::call(&s, cmd); })',
717	                file=self.f,
718	            )
719	
720	        if expr := metadata.string_expr:
721	            print(
722	                f'\t\t.def("__str__", [](const {basename} &s) {{ return {expr}; }})',
723	                file=self.f,
724	            )
725	            print(
726	                f'\t\t.def("__repr__", [](const {basename} &s) {{ std::stringstream ss; ss << "<{basename} " << {expr} << ">"; return ss.str(); }})',
727	                file=self.f,
728	            )
729	
730	        if expr := metadata.hash_expr:
731	            print(
732	                f'\t\t.def("__hash__", [](const {basename} &s) {{ return run_hash({expr}); }})',
733	                file=self.f,
734	            )
735	
736	        print(f"\t\t;}}", file=self.f)
737	
738	    def process_enum(
739	        self,
740	        enum: EnumDecl,
741	        namespace_components: Tuple[str, ...],
742	    ):
743	        pqname: PQName = enum.typename
744	        full_path = list(namespace_components) + [
745	            segment.format() for segment in pqname.segments
746	        ]
747	        basename = full_path.pop()
748	
749	        declaration_namespace = "::".join(full_path)
750	        print(
751	            f'\t\t{{using namespace {declaration_namespace}; py::native_enum<{basename}>(m, "{basename}", "enum.Enum")',
752	            file=self.f,
753	        )
754	        enum_class = enum.typename.classkey == "enum class"
755	        for value in enum.values:
756	            enum_class_qualifier = f"{basename}::" * enum_class
757	            print(
758	                f'\t\t\t.value("{value.name}", {enum_class_qualifier}{value.name})',
759	                file=self.f,
760	            )
761	        print(f"\t\t\t.finalize();}}", file=self.f)
762	
763	    def process_namespace(
764	        self,
765	        header: PyosysHeader,
766	        ns: NamespaceScope,
767	        namespace_components: Tuple[str, ...] = (),
768	    ):
769	        for name, subns in ns.namespaces.items():
770	            self.process_namespace(header, subns, (*namespace_components, name))
771	        if len(namespace_components) and (len(ns.functions) + len(ns.variables)):
772	            # TODO: Not essential but maybe move namespace usage into
773	            # process_function for consistency?
774	            print(
775	                f"\t\t{{ using namespace {'::'.join(namespace_components)};",
776	                file=self.f,
777	            )
778	            for function in ns.functions:
779	                self.process_function(function)
780	            for variable in ns.variables:
781	                self.process_variable(variable)
782	            print(f"\t\t}}", file=self.f)
783	        for enum in ns.enums:
784	            self.process_enum(enum, namespace_components)
785	        for cls in ns.classes:
786	            pqname = cls.class_decl.typename
787	            declared_name_str = pqname.segments[-1].format()
788	            if cls_metadata := header.classes_by_name.get(declared_name_str):
789	                self.process_class(cls_metadata, cls, namespace_components)
790	
791	
792	keyword_aliases = {
793	    "False": "False_",
794	    "None": "None_",
795	    "True": "True_",
796	    "and": "and_",
797	    "as": "as_",
798	    "assert": "assert_",
799	    "break": "break_",
800	    "class": "class_",
801	    "continue": "continue_",
802	    "def": "def_",
803	    "del": "del_",
804	    "elif": "elif_",
805	    "else": "else_",
806	    "except": "except_",
807	    "for": "for_",
808	    "from": "from_",
809	    "global": "global_",
810	    "if": "if_",
811	    "import": "import_",
812	    "in": "in_",
813	    "is": "is_",
814	    "lambda": "lambda_",
815	    "nonlocal": "nonlocal_",
816	    "not": "not_",
817	    "or": "or_",
818	    "pass": "pass_",
819	    "raise": "raise_",
820	    "return": "return_",
821	    "try": "try_",
822	    "while": "while_",
823	    "with": "with_",
824	    "yield": "yield_",
825	}
826	
827	
828	if __name__ == "__main__":
829	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
830	    ap.add_argument("--debug", default=0, type=int)
831	    ap.add_argument("--build-dir", default=".", type=str)
832	    group = ap.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
833	    group.add_argument("output", nargs="?")
834	    ns = ap.parse_args()
835	    build_dir = Path(ns.build_dir)
836	    out_path = Path(ns.output)
837	    out_inc = out_path.parent / (out_path.stem + ".inc.cc")
838	    out_dep = out_path.parent / (out_path.name + ".d")
839	    with (open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf8") as f,
840	          open(out_inc,  "w", encoding="utf8") as inc_f,
841	          open(out_dep,  "w", encoding="utf8") as dep_f):
842	        generator = PyosysWrapperGenerator(
843	            from_headers=pyosys_headers, wrapper_stream=f, header_stream=inc_f
844	        )
845	        generator.generate(ns.build_dir)
846	        for header in pyosys_headers:
847	            print(f"{out_path} {out_inc}: {__yosys_root__ / header.name}", file=dep_f)
848

#!/usr/bin/env python3
#  yosys -- Yosys Open SYnthesis Suite
#
#  Copyright (C) 2012  Claire Xenia Wolf <claire@yosyshq.com>
#
#  Permission to use, copy, modify, and/or distribute this software for any
#  purpose with or without fee is hereby granted, provided that the above
#  copyright notice and this permission notice appear in all copies.
#
#  THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND THE AUTHOR DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES
#  WITH REGARD TO THIS SOFTWARE INCLUDING ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF
#  MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHOR BE LIABLE FOR
#  ANY SPECIAL, DIRECT, INDIRECT, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OR ANY DAMAGES
#  WHATSOEVER RESULTING FROM LOSS OF USE, DATA OR PROFITS, WHETHER IN AN
#  ACTION OF CONTRACT, NEGLIGENCE OR OTHER TORTIOUS ACTION, ARISING OUT OF
#  OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THIS SOFTWARE.
#
#  Written by Mohamed Gaber <me@donn.website>
#
#  Inspired by py_wrap_generator.py by Benedikt Tutzer
"""
This generates:
- Wrapper calls for opaque container types
- Full wrappers for select classes and all enums, global functions and global
  variables

Jump to "MARK: Inclusion and Exclusion" to control the above.

Please run ruff on this file in particular to make sure it parses with Python
<= 3.12. There is so much f-string use here that the outer quote thing
is a common problem. ``ruff check pyosys/generator.py`` suffices.
"""
import os
import io
import shutil
import argparse
from pathlib import Path
from sysconfig import get_paths
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Dict, FrozenSet, Iterable, Tuple, Union, Optional, List

import pybind11
from cxxheaderparser.simple import parse_file, ClassScope, NamespaceScope, EnumDecl
from cxxheaderparser.options import ParserOptions
from cxxheaderparser.preprocessor import make_gcc_preprocessor
from cxxheaderparser.types import (
    PQName,
    Type,
    Pointer,
    Reference,
    MoveReference,
    AnonymousName,
    Method,
    Function,
    Field,
    Variable,
    Array,
    FundamentalSpecifier,
    FunctionType,
)

__file_dir__ = Path(__file__).absolute().parent
__yosys_root__ = __file_dir__.parent


@dataclass
class PyosysClass:
    """
    Metadata about classes or structs intended to be wrapped using Pyosys.

    :param name: The base name of the class (without extra qualifiers)
    :param ref_only: Whether this class can be copied to Python, or only
        referenced.
    :param string_expr:
        A C++ expression that will be used for the ``__str__`` method in Python.

        The object will be available as a const reference with the identifier
        `s`.
    :param hash_expr:
        A C++ expression that will be fed to ``run_hash`` to declare a
        ``__hash__`` method for Python.

        The object will be vailable as a const reference with the identifier
        `s`.
    :param denylist: If specified, one or more methods can be excluded from
        wrapping.
    """

    name: str
    ref_only: bool = False

    # in the format s.(method or property) (or just s)
    string_expr: Optional[str] = None
    hash_expr: Optional[str] = None

    denylist: FrozenSet[str] = frozenset({})


@dataclass
class PyosysHeader:
    """
    :param name: The name of the header, i.e., its relative path to the Yosys root
    :param classes: A list of ``PyosysClass`` classes to be wrapped
    :param enums: A list of enums to be wrapped
    """

    name: str
    classes: List[PyosysClass] = field(default_factory=lambda: [])

    def __post_init__(self):
        self.classes_by_name = {}
        if classes := self.classes:
            for cls in classes:
                self.classes_by_name[cls.name] = cls


# MARK: Inclusion and Exclusion
global_denylist = frozenset(
    {
        # deprecated
        "builtin_ff_cell_types",
        "logv_file_error",
        # no implementation
        "set_verific_logging",
        # can't bridge to python cleanly
        ## std::regex
        "log_warn_regexes",
        "log_nowarn_regexes",
        "log_werror_regexes",
        ## function pointers
        "log_error_atexit",
        "log_verific_callback",
        # incomplete type
        "yosys_get_tcl_interp",
    }
)
pyosys_headers = [
    # Headers for incomplete types
    PyosysHeader("kernel/binding.h"),
    PyosysHeader("libs/sha1/sha1.h"),
    # Headers for globals
    PyosysHeader("kernel/log.h"),
    PyosysHeader("kernel/yosys.h"),
    PyosysHeader("kernel/cost.h"),
    # Headers with classes
    PyosysHeader(
        "kernel/celltypes.h",
        [PyosysClass("CellType", hash_expr="s.type"), PyosysClass("CellTypes")],
    ),
    PyosysHeader("kernel/consteval.h", [PyosysClass("ConstEval")]),
    PyosysHeader(
        "kernel/register.h",
        [
            # PyosysClass("Pass") # Virtual methods, manually bridged
        ],
    ),
    PyosysHeader(
        "kernel/rtlil.h",
        [
            PyosysClass(
                "IdString",
                string_expr="s.str()",
                hash_expr="s.str()",
                denylist=frozenset(
                    # shouldn't be messed with from python in general
                    {
                        "global_id_storage_",
                        "global_id_index_",
                        "global_autoidx_id_storage_",
                        "global_refcount_storage_",
                        "global_free_idx_list_",
                        "builtin_ff_cell_types",
                        "substrings",
                    }
                ),
            ),
            PyosysClass(
                "Const",
                string_expr="s.as_string()",
                denylist=frozenset({"bits", "bitvectorize"}),
            ),
            PyosysClass("AttrObject", denylist=frozenset({"get_blackbox_attribute"})),
            PyosysClass("NamedObject"),
            PyosysClass("Selection"),
            # PyosysClass("Monitor"), # Virtual methods, manually bridged
            PyosysClass("CaseRule"),
            PyosysClass("SwitchRule"),
            PyosysClass("SyncRule"),
            PyosysClass(
                "Process",
                ref_only=True,
                string_expr="s.name.c_str()",
                hash_expr="s.name",
            ),
            PyosysClass("SigChunk"),
            PyosysClass("SigBit", hash_expr="s"),
            PyosysClass("SigSpec", hash_expr="s", denylist=frozenset({"chunks"})),
            PyosysClass(
                "Cell",
                ref_only=True,
                string_expr="s.name.c_str()",
                hash_expr="s",
            ),
            PyosysClass(
                "Wire",
                ref_only=True,
                string_expr="s.name.c_str()",
                hash_expr="s",
            ),
            PyosysClass(
                "Memory",
                ref_only=True,
                string_expr="s.name.c_str()",
                hash_expr="s",
            ),
            PyosysClass(
                "Module",
                ref_only=True,
                string_expr="s.name.c_str()",
                hash_expr="s",
                denylist=frozenset({"Pow"}),  # has no implementation
            ),
            PyosysClass(
                "Design",
                string_expr="std::to_string(s.hashidx_)",
                hash_expr="s",
                denylist=frozenset({"selected_whole_modules"}),  # deprecated
            ),
        ],
    ),
]


@dataclass(frozen=True)  # hashable
class PyosysType:
    """
    Bit of a hacky object all-around: this is more or less used to encapsulate
    container types so they can be later made opaque using pybind.
    """

    base: str
    specialization: Tuple["PyosysType", ...]
    const: bool = False

    @classmethod
    def from_type(Self, type_obj, drop_const=False) -> "PyosysType":
        const = hasattr(type_obj, "const") and type_obj.const and not drop_const
        if isinstance(type_obj, Pointer):
            ptr_to = Self.from_type(type_obj.ptr_to)
            return Self("ptr", (ptr_to,), const)
        elif isinstance(type_obj, Reference):
            ref_to = Self.from_type(type_obj.ref_to)
            return Self("ref", (ref_to,), const)
        elif isinstance(type_obj, FunctionType):
            ret_type = Self.from_type(type_obj.return_type)
            param_types = (Self.from_type(p.type) for p in type_obj.parameters)
            return Self("fn", (ret_type, *param_types), False)
        assert isinstance(
            type_obj, Type
        ), f"unexpected c++ type object of type {type(type_obj)}"
        last_segment = type_obj.typename.segments[-1]
        base = last_segment.name
        specialization = tuple()
        if (
            hasattr(last_segment, "specialization")
            and last_segment.specialization is not None
        ):
            for template_arg in last_segment.specialization.args:
                specialization = (*specialization, Self.from_type(template_arg.arg))
        return Self(base, specialization, const)

    def generate_identifier(self):
        title = self.base.title()
        if len(self.specialization) == 0:
            return title

        if title == "Dict":
            key, value = self.specialization
            return f"{key.generate_identifier()}To{value.generate_identifier()}{title}"
        elif title == "Fn":
            identifier = self.specialization[0].generate_identifier()
            if identifier == "Void":
                identifier = ""
            else:
                identifier += "From"
            identifier += "And".join(
                p.generate_identifier() for p in self.specialization[1:]
            )
            return identifier

        return (
            "".join(spec.generate_identifier() for spec in self.specialization) + title
        )

    def generate_cpp_name(self):
        const_prefix = "const " * self.const
        if len(self.specialization) == 0:
            return const_prefix + self.base
        elif self.base == "ptr":
            return const_prefix + f"{self.specialization[0].generate_cpp_name()} *"
        elif self.base == "ref":
            return const_prefix + f"{self.specialization[0].generate_cpp_name()} &"
        elif self.base == "fn":
            param_cpp_names = (s.generate_cpp_name() for s in self.specialization[1:])
            return f"{self.specialization[0].generate_cpp_name()}({','.join(param_cpp_names)})"
        else:
            return (
                const_prefix
                + f"{self.base}<{', '.join(spec.generate_cpp_name() for spec in self.specialization)}>"
            )


class PyosysWrapperGenerator(object):
    def __init__(
        self,
        from_headers: Iterable[PyosysHeader],
        wrapper_stream: io.TextIOWrapper,
        header_stream: io.TextIOWrapper,
    ):
        self.headers = from_headers
        self.f = wrapper_stream
        self.f_inc = header_stream
        self.found_containers: Dict[PyosysType, Any] = {}
        self.class_registry: Dict[str, Tuple[ClassScope, PyosysClass]] = {}

    # entry point
    def generate(self, build_dir):
        tpl = __file_dir__ / "wrappers_tpl.cc"
        preprocessor_opts = self.make_preprocessor_options(build_dir)
        with open(tpl, encoding="utf8") as f:
            do_line_directive = True
            for i, line in enumerate(f):
                if do_line_directive:
                    self.f.write(f'#line {i + 1} "{tpl}"\n')
                    do_line_directive = False
                if "<!-- generated includes -->" in line:
                    for header in self.headers:
                        self.f.write(f'#include "{header.name}"\n')
                    do_line_directive = True
                elif "<!-- generated pymod-level code -->" in line:
                    for header in self.headers:
                        header_path = __yosys_root__ / header.name
                        parsed = parse_file(header_path, options=preprocessor_opts)
                        global_namespace = parsed.namespace
                        self.process_namespace(header, global_namespace)
                else:
                    self.f.write(line)

        for container, _ in self.found_containers.items():
            identifier = container.generate_identifier()
            print(
                f"using {identifier} = {container.generate_cpp_name()};",
                file=self.f_inc,
            )
            print(f"PYBIND11_MAKE_OPAQUE({identifier})", file=self.f_inc)

        print(
            f"static void bind_autogenerated_opaque_containers(py::module &m) {{",
            file=self.f_inc,
        )
        for container, _ in self.found_containers.items():
            identifier = container.generate_identifier()
            cxx = container.generate_cpp_name()
            tpl_args = [cxx]
            for spec in container.specialization:
                tpl_args.append(spec.generate_cpp_name())
            print(
                f'\tpy::hashlib::bind_{container.base}<{", ".join(tpl_args)}>(m, "{container.generate_identifier()}");',
                file=self.f_inc,
            )
            print(
                f"\tpy::implicitly_convertible<py::iterable, {identifier}>();",
                file=self.f_inc,
            )
        print(f"}}", file=self.f_inc)

    # helpers
    def make_preprocessor_options(self, build_dir):
        py_include = get_paths()["include"]
        cxx_bin = os.getenv("CXX") or shutil.which("clang++") or "c++"
        if ccache := os.getenv("CCACHE"):
            compiler = [ccache, cxx_bin]
        else:
            compiler = [cxx_bin]
        cxx_std = os.getenv("CXX_STD", "c++20")
        return ParserOptions(
            preprocessor=make_gcc_preprocessor(
                defines=["_YOSYS_", "YOSYS_ENABLE_PYTHON"],
                gcc_args=[*compiler, f"-std={cxx_std}", "-fsyntax-only"],
                include_paths=[str(__yosys_root__), py_include, pybind11.get_include(), build_dir],
            ),
        )

    @staticmethod
    def find_containers(
        containers: Iterable[str], type_info: Any
    ) -> Dict[PyosysType, Any]:
        if isinstance(type_info, Pointer):
            return PyosysWrapperGenerator.find_containers(containers, type_info.ptr_to)
        if isinstance(type_info, MoveReference):
            return PyosysWrapperGenerator.find_containers(
                containers, type_info.moveref_to
            )
        if isinstance(type_info, Reference):
            return PyosysWrapperGenerator.find_containers(containers, type_info.ref_to)
        if not isinstance(type_info, Type):
            return {}
        segments = type_info.typename.segments
        containers_found = {}
        for segment in segments:
            if isinstance(segment, FundamentalSpecifier):
                continue
            if segment.name in containers:
                containers_found.update(
                    {PyosysType.from_type(type_info, drop_const=True): type_info}
                )
            if segment.specialization is not None:
                for arg in segment.specialization.args:
                    sub_containers = PyosysWrapperGenerator.find_containers(
                        containers, arg.arg
                    )
                    containers_found.update(sub_containers)
        return containers_found

    @staticmethod
    def find_anonymous_union(cls: ClassScope):
        if cls.class_decl.typename.classkey != "union":
            return None
        for s in cls.class_decl.typename.segments:
            if isinstance(s, AnonymousName):
                return s
        return None  # named union

    @staticmethod
    def get_parameter_types(function: Function) -> str:
        return ", ".join(p.type.format() for p in function.parameters)

    def register_containers(self, target: Union[Function, Field, Variable]) -> bool:
        supported = ("dict", "idict", "pool", "set", "vector")
        found = False
        if isinstance(target, Function):
            return_type_containers = self.find_containers(supported, target.return_type)
            found = found or len(return_type_containers)
            self.found_containers.update(return_type_containers)

            for parameter in target.parameters:
                parameter_containers = self.find_containers(supported, parameter.type)
                found = found or len(parameter_containers)
                self.found_containers.update(parameter_containers)
        else:
            variable_containers = self.find_containers(supported, target.type)
            found = found or len(variable_containers)
            self.found_containers.update(variable_containers)
        return found

    # processors
    def get_overload_cast(
        self, function: Function, class_basename: Optional[str]
    ) -> str:
        is_method = isinstance(function, Method)
        function_return_type = function.return_type.format()
        if class_basename in {"Const","IdString"} and function_return_type in {
            "iterator",
            "const_iterator",
        }:
            # HACK: qualify Const's iterators
            function_return_type = f"{class_basename}::{function_return_type}"

        pointer_kind = (
            f"{class_basename}::*" if (is_method and not function.static) else "*"
        )

        retval = f"static_cast <"
        retval += function_return_type
        retval += f"({pointer_kind})"
        retval += f"({self.get_parameter_types(function)})"
        if is_method and function.const:
            retval += " const"
        retval += ">"
        retval += "(&"
        if is_method:
            retval += f"{class_basename}::"
        retval += function.name.segments[-1].format()
        retval += ")"
        return retval

    def get_definition_args(
        self,
        function: Function,
        class_basename: Optional[str],
        python_name_override: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> List[str]:
        function_basename = function.name.segments[-1].format()

        python_function_basename = python_name_override or keyword_aliases.get(
            function_basename, function_basename
        )

        def_args = [f'"{python_function_basename}"']
        def_args.append(self.get_overload_cast(function, class_basename))
        for i, parameter in enumerate(function.parameters):
            name = parameter.name or f"arg{i}"
            parameter_arg = f'py::arg("{name}")'
            if parameter.default is not None:
                parameter_arg += f" = {parameter.default.format()}"
            def_args.append(parameter_arg)

        return def_args

    def process_method(self, metadata: PyosysClass, function: Method):
        if (
            function.deleted
            or function.template
            or function.vararg
            or function.access != "public"
            or function.pure_virtual
            or function.destructor
        ):
            return

        if any(isinstance(p.type, MoveReference) for p in function.parameters):
            # skip move constructors
            return

        if len(function.name.segments) > 1:
            # can't handle, skip
            return

        if function.constructor:
            if (
                not metadata.ref_only
            ):  # ref-only classes should not be constructed from python
                print(
                    f"\t\t\t.def(py::init<{self.get_parameter_types(function)}>())",
                    file=self.f,
                )
            return

        python_name_override = None
        if function.operator is not None:
            if function.operator == "==":
                python_name_override = "__eq__"
            elif function.operator == "!=":
                python_name_override = "__ne__"
            elif function.operator == "<":
                python_name_override = "__lt__"
            elif function.operator == "[]" and function.const:
                python_name_override = "__getitem__"
            else:
                return

        self.register_containers(function)

        definition_fn = "def"
        if function.static:
            definition_fn = "def_static"

        definition_args = self.get_definition_args(
            function, metadata.name, python_name_override
        )

        print(
            f"\t\t\t.{definition_fn}({', '.join(definition_args)})",
            file=self.f,
        )

    def process_function(self, function: Function):
        if function.deleted or function.template or function.vararg or function.static:
            return

        if function.operator is not None:
            # Python doesn't do global operators
            return

        if function.name.segments[-1].format() in global_denylist:
            return

        self.register_containers(function)

        print(
            f"\t\t\tm.def({', '.join(self.get_definition_args(function, None))});",
            file=self.f,
        )

    def process_field(self, metadata: PyosysClass, field: Field):
        if field.access != "public":
            return

        if field.name is None:
            # anonymous structs and unions
            # unions handled in calling function
            # ASSUMPTION: No anonymous structs in the yosys codebase
            # 			  (they're not in the C++ standard anyway)
            return

        unique_ptrs = self.find_containers(("unique_ptr",), field.type)
        if len(unique_ptrs):
            # TODO: figure out how to bridge unique pointers maybe does anyone
            # care
            return

        self.register_containers(field)
        rvp = "py::return_value_policy::copy"
        if isinstance(field.type, Pointer):
            rvp = "py::return_value_policy::reference_internal"

        definition_fn = f"def_{'readonly' if field.type.const else 'readwrite'}"
        if field.static:
            definition_fn += "_static"

        field_python_basename = keyword_aliases.get(field.name, field.name)

        def_args = [
            f'"{field_python_basename}"',
            f"&{metadata.name}::{field.name}",
        ]
        def_args.append(rvp)
        print(
            f"\t\t\t.{definition_fn}({', '.join(def_args)})",
            file=self.f,
        )

    def process_variable(self, variable: Variable):
        if isinstance(variable.type, Array):
            return

        variable_basename = variable.name.segments[-1].format()
        if variable_basename in global_denylist:
            return

        self.register_containers(variable)

        definition_fn = (
            f"def_{'readonly' if (variable.type.const or variable.constexpr) else 'readwrite'}_static"
        )

        variable_python_basename = keyword_aliases.get(
            variable_basename, variable_basename
        )
        variable_name = variable.name.format()

        print(
            f'\t\t\tglobal_variables.{definition_fn}("{variable_python_basename}", &{variable_name});',
            file=self.f,
        )

    def process_class_members(
        self,
        metadata: PyosysClass,
        base_metadata: PyosysClass,
        cls: ClassScope,
        basename: str,
    ):
        for method in cls.methods:
            if method.name.segments[-1].name in base_metadata.denylist:
                continue
            self.process_method(metadata, method)

        visited_anonymous_unions = set()
        for field_ in cls.fields:
            if field_.name in base_metadata.denylist:
                continue
            self.process_field(metadata, field_)

            # Handle anonymous unions
            for subclass in cls.classes:
                if subclass.class_decl.access != "public":
                    continue
                if au := self.find_anonymous_union(subclass):
                    if au.id in visited_anonymous_unions:
                        continue
                    visited_anonymous_unions.add(au.id)
                    for subfield in subclass.fields:
                        self.process_field(metadata, subfield)

        for base in cls.class_decl.bases:
            if base.access != "public":
                continue
            name = base.typename.segments[-1].format()
            if processed := self.class_registry.get(name):
                base_scope, base_metadata = processed
                self.process_class_members(
                    metadata, base_metadata, base_scope, basename
                )

    def process_class(
        self,
        metadata: PyosysClass,
        cls: ClassScope,
        namespace_components: Tuple[str, ...],
    ):
        pqname: PQName = cls.class_decl.typename
        full_path = list(namespace_components) + [
            segment.format() for segment in pqname.segments
        ]
        basename = full_path.pop()
        self.class_registry[basename] = (cls, metadata)

        declaration_namespace = "::".join(full_path)
        tpl_args = [basename]
        if metadata.ref_only:
            tpl_args.append(f"std::unique_ptr<{basename}, py::nodelete>")
        print(
            f'\t\t{{using namespace {declaration_namespace}; py::class_<{", ".join(tpl_args)}>(m, "{basename}")',
            file=self.f,
        )

        self.process_class_members(metadata, metadata, cls, basename)

        if basename == "Design":
            print(
                '\t\t\t.def("run_pass", [](Design &s, std::vector<std::string> cmd) { Pass::call(&s, cmd); })',
                file=self.f,
            )
            print(
                '\t\t\t.def("run_pass", [](Design &s, std::string cmd) { Pass::call(&s, cmd); })',
                file=self.f,
            )

        if expr := metadata.string_expr:
            print(
                f'\t\t.def("__str__", [](const {basename} &s) {{ return {expr}; }})',
                file=self.f,
            )
            print(
                f'\t\t.def("__repr__", [](const {basename} &s) {{ std::stringstream ss; ss << "<{basename} " << {expr} << ">"; return ss.str(); }})',
                file=self.f,
            )

        if expr := metadata.hash_expr:
            print(
                f'\t\t.def("__hash__", [](const {basename} &s) {{ return run_hash({expr}); }})',
                file=self.f,
            )

        print(f"\t\t;}}", file=self.f)

    def process_enum(
        self,
        enum: EnumDecl,
        namespace_components: Tuple[str, ...],
    ):
        pqname: PQName = enum.typename
        full_path = list(namespace_components) + [
            segment.format() for segment in pqname.segments
        ]
        basename = full_path.pop()

        declaration_namespace = "::".join(full_path)
        print(
            f'\t\t{{using namespace {declaration_namespace}; py::native_enum<{basename}>(m, "{basename}", "enum.Enum")',
            file=self.f,
        )
        enum_class = enum.typename.classkey == "enum class"
        for value in enum.values:
            enum_class_qualifier = f"{basename}::" * enum_class
            print(
                f'\t\t\t.value("{value.name}", {enum_class_qualifier}{value.name})',
                file=self.f,
            )
        print(f"\t\t\t.finalize();}}", file=self.f)

    def process_namespace(
        self,
        header: PyosysHeader,
        ns: NamespaceScope,
        namespace_components: Tuple[str, ...] = (),
    ):
        for name, subns in ns.namespaces.items():
            self.process_namespace(header, subns, (*namespace_components, name))
        if len(namespace_components) and (len(ns.functions) + len(ns.variables)):
            # TODO: Not essential but maybe move namespace usage into
            # process_function for consistency?
            print(
                f"\t\t{{ using namespace {'::'.join(namespace_components)};",
                file=self.f,
            )
            for function in ns.functions:
                self.process_function(function)
            for variable in ns.variables:
                self.process_variable(variable)
            print(f"\t\t}}", file=self.f)
        for enum in ns.enums:
            self.process_enum(enum, namespace_components)
        for cls in ns.classes:
            pqname = cls.class_decl.typename
            declared_name_str = pqname.segments[-1].format()
            if cls_metadata := header.classes_by_name.get(declared_name_str):
                self.process_class(cls_metadata, cls, namespace_components)


keyword_aliases = {
    "False": "False_",
    "None": "None_",
    "True": "True_",
    "and": "and_",
    "as": "as_",
    "assert": "assert_",
    "break": "break_",
    "class": "class_",
    "continue": "continue_",
    "def": "def_",
    "del": "del_",
    "elif": "elif_",
    "else": "else_",
    "except": "except_",
    "for": "for_",
    "from": "from_",
    "global": "global_",
    "if": "if_",
    "import": "import_",
    "in": "in_",
    "is": "is_",
    "lambda": "lambda_",
    "nonlocal": "nonlocal_",
    "not": "not_",
    "or": "or_",
    "pass": "pass_",
    "raise": "raise_",
    "return": "return_",
    "try": "try_",
    "while": "while_",
    "with": "with_",
    "yield": "yield_",
}


if __name__ == "__main__":
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--debug", default=0, type=int)
    ap.add_argument("--build-dir", default=".", type=str)
    group = ap.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
    group.add_argument("output", nargs="?")
    ns = ap.parse_args()
    build_dir = Path(ns.build_dir)
    out_path = Path(ns.output)
    out_inc = out_path.parent / (out_path.stem + ".inc.cc")
    out_dep = out_path.parent / (out_path.name + ".d")
    with (open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf8") as f,
          open(out_inc,  "w", encoding="utf8") as inc_f,
          open(out_dep,  "w", encoding="utf8") as dep_f):
        generator = PyosysWrapperGenerator(
            from_headers=pyosys_headers, wrapper_stream=f, header_stream=inc_f
        )
        generator.generate(ns.build_dir)
        for header in pyosys_headers:
            print(f"{out_path} {out_inc}: {__yosys_root__ / header.name}", file=dep_f)

import os
import sys

sys.setdlopenflags(os.RTLD_NOW | os.RTLD_GLOBAL)

__dir__ = os.path.abspath(os.path.dirname(__file__))
sys._pyosys_dir = os.path.abspath(__dir__)

bin_ext = ".exe" if os.name == "nt" else ""

_share_candidate = os.path.join(__dir__, "share")
if os.path.isdir(_share_candidate):
    sys._pyosys_share_dirname = _share_candidate + os.path.sep

_abc_candidate = os.path.join(__dir__, f"yosys-abc{bin_ext}")
if os.path.isfile(_abc_candidate):
    sys._pyosys_abc = _abc_candidate

__all__ = ["libyosys"]

add_custom_command(
	DEPENDS
		${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/generator.py
		${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/wrappers_tpl.cc
	OUTPUT
		${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/wrappers.cc
		${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/wrappers.inc.cc
	DEPFILE
		${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/wrappers.cc.d
	COMMAND
		${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E env
			CCACHE=${CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER}
			CXX=${CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER}
			CXX_STD=c++${CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD}
		-- ${PyosysEnv_PYTHON}
			${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/generator.py
			${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/wrappers.cc
			--build-dir ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}
	VERBATIM
	COMMENT "Generating Python wrappers"
)
yosys_core(pyosys
	wrappers.cc
	INCLUDE_DIRS
		${pybind11_INCLUDE_DIR}
	LIBRARIES
		$<${YOSYS_ENABLE_PYTHON}:Python3::Module>
	ESSENTIAL
)

file.content:
# To build a wheel with additional CMake options, use `--build-option`, e.g.:
#
# 	python -m build -w -Ccmake=-DYOSYS_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache
#   pip install -Ccmake=-DYOSYS_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache .

import re
import os
import sys
import pathlib
import tarfile
import tempfile
import subprocess
import sysconfig
from email.policy import EmailPolicy
from email.message import EmailMessage
from wheel.wheelfile import WheelFile


PROJECT_NAME = "pyosys"
PROJECT_VERSION = subprocess.check_output([
	"cmake",
	f"-DCMAKE_SOURCE_DIR={os.getcwd()}",
	"-P", "cmake/GetPyosysVersion.cmake"
], encoding="ascii").strip()
DIST_NAME = f"{PROJECT_NAME}-{PROJECT_VERSION}"


# https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/platform-compatibility-tags/
if sys.implementation.name == "cpython":
	PYTHON_TAG = f"cp{sysconfig.get_config_var('py_version_nodot')}"
	# freethreaded builds have an ABI flag appended, "t"
	ABI_TAG = f"cp{sysconfig.get_config_var('py_version_nodot')}{sysconfig.get_config_var('abiflags')}"
else:
	raise NotImplementedError("unsupported Python implementation")
# get_platform() always returns the MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET this intepreter is
# configured with:
# 	https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/494f2e3c92cc1b7774cca16fca5c7d1ff18c0de2/Lib/_osx_support.py#L504
PLATFORM_TAG_RAW = sysconfig.get_platform()
MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET_FLAGS = []
if interpreter_deployment_target := sysconfig.get_config_var("MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET"):
	cmake_deployment_target = interpreter_deployment_target
	interpreter_deployment_target = tuple(int(v) for v in interpreter_deployment_target.split("."))
	# Yosys fails to compile for anything below 10.15 because of std::filesystem
	requested_deployment_target = tuple(int(v) for v in os.environ.get("MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET", "10.15").split("."))
	if requested_deployment_target > interpreter_deployment_target:
		resolved_platform_version_string = ".".join(str(v) for v in requested_deployment_target)
		cmake_deployment_target = resolved_platform_version_string
		if "." not in resolved_platform_version_string:
			# macOS 11+ need to be "bare" for MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET but have
			# the .0 for Python platform versions
			resolved_platform_version_string += ".0"
		PLATFORM_TAG_RAW = re.sub(r"(macosx)-\d+\.\d+", rf"\1-{resolved_platform_version_string}", PLATFORM_TAG_RAW)
	MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET_FLAGS = [f"-DCMAKE_OSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET={cmake_deployment_target}"]
# Source for these substitutions:
# 	https://github.com/pypa/wheel/blob/197012dcb8a9da10570d6486bc1a70305861e7f2/src/wheel/_bdist_wheel.py#L351
PLATFORM_TAG = PLATFORM_TAG_RAW.lower().replace("-", "_").replace(".", "_").replace(" ", "_")
COMPAT_TAG = f"{PYTHON_TAG}-{ABI_TAG}-{PLATFORM_TAG}"


def compile_pyosys(cmake_options=[], parallel=os.cpu_count() or 1):
	install_dir = tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="pyosys_install")
	with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="pyosys_build") as build_dir:
		subprocess.check_call([
			"cmake",
			"-S", ".",
			"-B", build_dir,
			"-DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release",
			f"-DPython3_EXECUTABLE={sys.executable}",
			"-DYOSYS_WITH_PYTHON=ON",
			"-DYOSYS_INSTALL_DRIVER=OFF",
			"-DYOSYS_INSTALL_LIBRARY=OFF",
			"-DYOSYS_INSTALL_PYTHON=ON",
			f"-DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX={install_dir.name}",
			f"-DYOSYS_INSTALL_PYTHON_SITEDIR=python",
			"-DYOSYS_BUILD_PYTHON_ONLY=ON",
			*cmake_options,
			*MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET_FLAGS,
		])
		subprocess.check_call([
			"cmake",
			"--build", build_dir,
			"-t", "pyosys",
			f"-j{parallel}",
		])
		subprocess.check_call([
			"cmake",
			"--install", build_dir,
			"--strip",
		])
	return install_dir


def make_message(headers, payload=None):
	msg = EmailMessage(policy=EmailPolicy(max_line_length=0))
	for name, value in headers:
		if isinstance(value, list):
			for value_part in value:
				msg[name] = value_part
		else:
			msg[name] = value
	if payload:
		msg.set_payload(payload)
	return bytes(msg)


def build_sdist(sdist_dir, config_settings=None):
	sdist_filename = f"{DIST_NAME}.tar.gz"

	with tarfile.open(pathlib.Path(sdist_dir) / sdist_filename, "w:gz",
			format=tarfile.PAX_FORMAT) as sdist:
		def exclude_build(entry):
			name = entry.name.removeprefix(f"{DIST_NAME}/")
			if name in (".cache", "build", "dist"):
				return
			if os.path.basename(name) in (".git", "__pycache__"):
				return
			return entry
		sdist.add(os.getcwd(), arcname=DIST_NAME, filter=exclude_build)

	return sdist_filename


def get_metadata_files():
	with open("README.md", "rb") as readme:
		long_description = readme.read()

	return {
		"WHEEL": make_message([
			("Wheel-Version", "1.0"),
			("Generator", "pyosys build backend"),
			("Root-Is-Purelib", "false"),
			("Tag", [COMPAT_TAG]),
		]),
		"METADATA": make_message([
			("Metadata-Version", "2.4"),
			("Name", PROJECT_NAME),
			("Version", PROJECT_VERSION),
			("Summary", "Python access to libyosys"),
			("Description-Content-Type", "text/markdown"),
			("License-Expression", "MIT"),
			("Classifier", "Programming Language :: Python :: 3"),
			("Classifier", "Intended Audience :: Developers"),
			("Classifier", "Operating System :: POSIX :: Linux"),
			("Classifier", "Operating System :: MacOS :: MacOS X"),
			("Requires-Python", ">=3.10"),
		], long_description)
	}


def prepare_metadata_for_build_wheel(metadata_directory, config_settings=None):
	os.mkdir(f"{metadata_directory}/{DIST_NAME}.dist-info")

	for filename, contents in get_metadata_files().items():
		with open(f"{metadata_directory}/{DIST_NAME}.dist-info/{filename}", "wb") as f:
			f.write(contents)

	return f"{DIST_NAME}.dist-info"


def build_wheel(wheel_dir, config_settings=None, metadata_directory=None):
	wheel_filename = f"{DIST_NAME}-{COMPAT_TAG}.whl"

	with WheelFile(pathlib.Path(wheel_dir) / wheel_filename, "w") as wheel:
		for filename, contents in get_metadata_files().items():
			wheel.writestr(f"{DIST_NAME}.dist-info/{filename}", contents)

		cmake_options = []
		if config_settings is not None:
			if cmake_options := config_settings.get("cmake", cmake_options):
				if isinstance(cmake_options, str):
					cmake_options = [cmake_options]
		with compile_pyosys(cmake_options) as install_dir:
			wheel.write_files(pathlib.Path(install_dir) / "python")

	return wheel_filename

Now I have all the information I need. There's one concern: `cells()` and `wires()` return `RTLIL::ObjRange`, not `std::vector`. The generator might not handle `ObjRange` properly. Let me check if `cells()` would be exposed. Actually, `process_method` only skips if deleted/template/vararg/non-public/pure_virtual/destructor/move-ref/multi-segment-name. `cells()` returns ObjRange<RTLIL::Cell*>. The container registration handles dict/idict/pool/set/vector, not ObjRange. So `cells()` might be wrapped but return an opaque ObjRange that's hard to use from Python. The examples use `module.selected_cells()` which returns `std::vector<RTLIL::Cell*>`. 

For the practice task, the spec says: "加载 counter.v、综合、遍历 module 的 cells 打印每个 cell 的 type 与 port 连接。" So I should use `selected_cells()` or `cells()`. Since `selected_cells()` returns a `std::vector`, it's safer to iterate. But actually, the practice task wants to iterate "module 的 cells". Let me use the approach consistent with the examples — `module.selected_cells()` after a select, or better, iterate over all modules and cells.

Actually, looking more carefully: the example pass.py uses `design.all_selected_whole_modules()` and `module.selected_cells()`. The script.py uses `design.selected_whole_modules_warn()` and `module.selected_cells()`.

For the practice task, after `prep` + `opt`, the design will have selected modules. Let me use `design.selected_whole_modules_warn()` → `module.selected_cells()` then for each cell print `cell.type.str()` and `cell.connections()`.

Wait — `connections()` returns `const dict<IdString, SigSpec>&`. dict is wrapped as opaque container in hashlib bindings. So iterating it in Python... The hashlib.h has `bind_dict` etc. Let me check if dict is iterable in Python. Let me look at hashlib.h quickly.

Actually, for the practice task I want to keep it simple and accurate. Let me think about what definitely works:
- `cell.type.str()` — works (IdString has str() method, string_expr="s.str()")

aiTitle: Yosys Python 绑定 pyosys 讲义

content.thinking: There's official documentation at docs/source/using_yosys/pyosys.rst. Let me read it to ground the lecture in the official description.

content.text: 这里有官方文档。我先阅读一下，以便让本讲义有据可依。

content.content:
1	Scripting with Pyosys
2	=====================
3	
4	.. TODO:: document libyosys sans python
5	
6	Pyosys is a limited subset of the Yosys C++ API (aka "libyosys") made available
7	using the Python programming language.
8	
9	Like ``.ys`` and ``.tcl`` scripts, Pyosys provides an interface to write Yosys
10	scripts in the Python programming language, giving you the benefits of
11	a type system, control flow, object-oriented programming, and more; especially
12	that the other options lack a type system and control flow/OOP in Tcl is
13	limited.
14	
15	Though unlike these two, Pyosys goes a bit further, allowing you to use the
16	Yosys API to implement advanced functionality that would otherwise require
17	custom passes written in C++.
18	
19	.. note::
20	
21	   It is recommended to install :program:`uv` for managing python environments:
22	
23	   .. code:: console
24	
25	      curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
26	
27	Getting Pyosys
28	--------------
29	
30	Pyosys supports CPython 3.8 or higher. You can access Pyosys using one of two
31	methods:
32	
33	1. Compiling Yosys with the CMake flag ``-DYOSYS_WITH_PYTHON=ON``
34	
35	   This adds the flag ``-y`` to the Yosys binary, which allows you to execute
36	   Python scripts using an interpreter embedded in Yosys itself:
37	
38	   ``yosys -y ./my_pyosys_script.py``
39	
40	   Do note this requires some build-time dependencies to be available to Python,
41	   namely, ``pybind11`` and ``cxxheaderparser``. :program:`uv` may be used to
42	   create an ephemeral environment with the correct versions of the tools
43	   installed if the current python environment doesn't provide them.
44	
45	2. Installing the Pyosys wheels
46	
47	   On macOS and GNU/Linux you can install pre-built wheels of Yosys using
48	   ``pip``:
49	
50	   ``python3 -m pip install pyosys``
51	
52	   Which then allows you to run your scripts as follows:
53	
54	   ``python3 ./my_pyosys_script.py``
55	
56	
57	Scripting and Database Inspection
58	---------------------------------
59	
60	To start with, you have to import libyosys as follows:
61	
62	.. code-block:: python
63	
64	   from pyosys import libyosys
65	
66	
67	As a reminder, Python allows you to alias imported modules and objects, so
68	this import may be preferable for terseness:
69	
70	.. code-block:: python
71	
72	   from pyosys import libyosys as ys
73	
74	
75	Now, scripting is actually quite similar to ``.ys`` and ``.tcl`` script in that
76	you can provide mostly text commands. Albeit, you can construct your scripts
77	to use Python's amenities like conditional execution, loops, and functions:
78	
79	.. code-block:: python
80	
81	   do_flatten = True
82	
83	   ys.run_pass("read_verilog tests/simple/fiedler-cooley.v")
84	   ys.run_pass("hierarchy -check -auto-top")
85	   if do_flatten:
86	      ys.run_pass("flatten")
87	
88	…but this does not strictly provide anything that Tcl scripts do not provide you
89	with. The real power of using Pyosys comes from the fact you can manually
90	instantiate, manage, and interact with the design database.
91	
92	As an example, here is the same script with a manually instantiated design.
93	
94	.. literalinclude:: /code_examples/pyosys/simple_database.py
95	   :start-after: loading design
96	   :end-before: top module inspection
97	   :language: python
98	
99	What's new here is that you can manually inspect the design's database. This
100	gives you access to a huge chunk of the design database API as declared in
101	the ``kernel/rtlil.h`` header.
102	
103	For example, here's how to list the input and output ports of the top module
104	of your design:
105	
106	.. literalinclude:: /code_examples/pyosys/simple_database.py
107	   :start-after: top module inspection
108	   :end-before: # synth
109	   :language: python
110	
111	.. tip::
112	
113	   C++ data structures in Yosys are bridged to Python such that they have a
114	   pretty similar API to Python objects, for example:
115	
116	   - ``std::vector`` supports the same methods as
117	     `iterables <https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-iterable>`_ in
118	     Python.
119	   - ``std::set`` and hashlib ``pool`` support the same methods as ``set``\s in
120	     Python. While ``set`` is ordered, ``pool`` is not and modifications may
121	     cause a complete reordering of the set.
122	   - ``dict`` supports the same methods as ``dict``\s in Python, albeit it is
123	     unordered, and modifications may cause a complete reordering of the
124	     dictionary.
125	   - ``idict`` uses a custom set of methods because it doesn't map very cleanly
126	     to an existing Python data structure. See ``pyosys/hashlib.h`` for more
127	     info.
128	
129	   For most operations, the Python equivalents are also supported as arguments
130	   where they will automatically be cast to the right type, so you do not have
131	   to manually instantiate the right underlying C++ object(s) yourself.
132	
133	Modifying the Database
134	----------------------
135	
136	.. warning::
137	
138	   Any modifications to the database may invalidate previous references held
139	   by Python, just as if you were writing C++. Pyosys does not currently attempt
140	   to keep deleted objects alive if a reference is held by Python.
141	
142	You are not restricted to inspecting the database either: you have the ability
143	to modify it, and introduce new elements and/or changes to your design.
144	
145	As a demonstrative example, let's assume we want to add an enable line to all
146	flip-flops in our fiedler-cooley design.
147	
148	First of all, we will run :yoscrypt:`synth` to convert all of the logic to
149	Yosys's internal cell structure (see :ref:`sec:celllib_gates`):
150	
151	.. literalinclude:: /code_examples/pyosys/simple_database.py
152	   :start-after: # synth
153	   :end-before: adding the enable line
154	   :language: python
155	
156	Next, we need to add the new port. The method for this is ``Module::addWire``\.
157	
158	.. tip::
159	
160	   IdString is Yosys's internal representation of strings used as identifiers
161	   within Verilog designs. They are efficient as only integers are stored and
162	   passed around, but they can be translated to and from normal strings at will.
163	
164	   Pyosys will automatically cast Python strings to IdStrings for you, but the
165	   rules around IdStrings apply, namely that *broadly*:
166	
167	   - Identifiers for internal cells must start with ``$``\.
168	   - All other identifiers must start with ``\``\.
169	
170	.. literalinclude:: /code_examples/pyosys/simple_database.py
171	   :start-after: adding the enable line
172	   :end-before: hooking the enable line
173	   :language: python
174	
175	Notice how we modified the wire then called a method to make Yosys re-process
176	the ports.
177	
178	Next, we can iterate over all constituent cells, and if they are of the type
179	``$_DFF_P_``, we do two things:
180	
181	1. Change their type to ``$_DFFE_PP_`` to enable hooking up an enable signal.
182	2. Hooking up the enable signal.
183	
184	.. literalinclude:: /code_examples/pyosys/simple_database.py
185	   :start-after: hooking the enable line
186	   :end-before: run check
187	   :language: python
188	
189	To verify that you did everything correctly, it is prudent to call ``.check()``
190	on the module you're manipulating as follows after you're done with a set of
191	changes:
192	
193	.. literalinclude:: /code_examples/pyosys/simple_database.py
194	   :start-after: run check
195	   :end-before: write output
196	   :language: python
197	
198	And then finally, write your outputs. Here, I choose an intermediate Verilog
199	file and :yoscrypt:`synth_ice40` to map it to the iCE40 architecture.
200	
201	.. literalinclude:: /code_examples/pyosys/simple_database.py
202	   :start-after: write output
203	   :language: python
204	
205	And voilà, you will note that in the intermediate output, all ``always @``
206	statements should have an ``if (enable)``\.
207	
208	Encapsulating as Passes
209	-----------------------
210	
211	Just like when writing C++, you can encapsulate routines in terms of "passes",
212	which adds your Pass to a global registry of commands accessible using
213	``run_pass``\.
214	
215	.. literalinclude:: /code_examples/pyosys/pass.py
216	   :language: python
217	
218	In general, abstract classes and virtual methods are not really supported by
219	Pyosys due to their complexity, but there are two exceptions which are:
220	
221	- ``Pass`` in ``kernel/register.h``
222	- ``Monitor`` in ``kernel/rtlil.h``
223
